rulings.Case Title: WINSTON S NICHOLS ET AL VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 26, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is granted. There is no argument that defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to submit evidence sufficient to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' complaint, interrogatory responses and Mr. Nichols's deposition testimony are irrelevant to the issue. The admissible portions of the declaration of Barry Castleman and defendant's responses to standard interrogatories are not sufficient to create a triable issue. Even if Mr. Castleman's declaration may be considered in its entirety, while it may demonstrate that defendant acquired knowledge regarding the hazards of asbestos, it is not sufficient to demonstrate a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others. While plaintiffs do not need to prove their case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration and concludes that no reasonable jury could find plaintiffs' evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Defendant's objections are sustained as to: plaintiffs' responses to standard interrogatories and special interrogatories (plaintiffs' Exhibits 2 and 4) and paragraphs 29 through 34 of the declaration of Barry Castleman (plaintiff's Exhibit 5). Defendant's objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Nichols are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/LOT)

rulings.Case Title: WINSTON S NICHOLS ET AL VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 26, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Metalclad Insulation LLC's motion for summary adjudication: the Court's tentative ruling is to grant plaintiffs' request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. § 437c(h). A hearing is required to discuss a supplemental briefing schedule and continued hearing date. Additionally, the parties should be prepared to discuss a possible continuance of the trial date. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. =(503/LOT)
